description: Letter from Shelomo b. Yehuda very early in his public career, when he was Av Bet Din, to a notable in Fustat, ca. 1025. He signs: Shelomo Av ha-Yeshiva birabbi Yehuda, and surrounds his name with tiny letters spelling out ‘And I am a worm and not a man’ (Psalms 22:7). The letter was carried by his son Avraham (לידו מיד חמוד), and he is seeking help for the community of Jerusalem, many of whom have died in a plague (נאספו בדבר אשר נפל בעיר). (Information from CUDL)
tags: Shelomo b. Yehuda, epidemic, epidemic: dever, illness letter 969-1517, Illness
description_he: מכתב משלמה בן יהודה בתחילת כהונתו כאב בית דין לנכבד בפוסטאט, כ-1025. מבקש עזרה לקהילת ירושלים שרבים ממנה מתו במגפה. חותם: 'ואנכי תולעת ולא איש'.
